data.title: Alibaba integrates Qwen AI across ecosystem to handle user tasks
data.description:
• Alibaba Group (BABA) is launching a significant upgrade to its Qwen app, embedding the chatbot more deeply across its ecosystem and enabling it to complete tasks for users.
  • The update lets users ask Qwen to complete tasks like ordering food or booking flights directly, as the app is now integrated with Alibaba’s ecosystem, including Taobao and Fliggy.
  • “What we are launching today represents a shift from models that understand to systems that act—deeply connected to real-world services,” Wu Jia, a vice president at Alibaba, said during a launch event in Hangzhou, China, on Thursday.
  • The Qwen app also debuted a new “task assistant” feature, currently available for invited users.
  • The upgrade follows a major revamp of the Qwen app two months ago, marking Alibaba’s (BABA) strategic shift toward consumer-facing AI—an area where it had previously trailed domestic competitors ByteDance (BDNCE) and Tencent (TCEHY) while concentrating mainly on enterprise AI services via its cloud business.

data.title: Bristow Group prices $500M notes offering
data.description:
• Bristow Group (VTOL) has pricing of an upsized private offering of $500M aggregate principal amount of 6.75% senior secured notes due 2033.
  • The notes will mature on February 1, 2033, and will be issued at par. The company will set aside part of the offering proceeds with the trustee to fully redeem its 6.875% senior secured notes due 2028 on March 1, 2026, covering all principal, premium, interest, and related payments owed up to the redemption date.
  • As a result, the 2028 Notes Indenture will be satisfied and discharged in accordance with its terms with respect to the 2028 Notes.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on January 26, 2026, and is subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump tells Reuters he has no plan to fire Fed’s Powell despite probe
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that he has no plans to dismiss Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell despite a Justice Department criminal investigation, adding that it was “too early” to say what he might ultimately decide.

Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell is the target of a Justice Department investigation into his handling of the refurbishment of the Fed's headquarters.

Powell has said that the investigation is motivated by the White House's opposition to FOMC rate policy, something that the president and administration deny.

 “I don’t have any plan to do that,” Trump told Reuters in an interview on Wednesday when asked if he would remove Powell.

Asked whether the investigation gave him grounds to do so, Trump added: "Right now, we're (in) a little bit of a holding pattern with him, and we're going to determine what to do. But I can't get into it. It’s too soon. Too early.”

Powell’s term as Federal Reserve chair ends in May, though he can remain on the Washington-based Board of Governors until 2028.

Trump said he is considering former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh or National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett as potential successors, while ruling out Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, saying he prefers to remain in his current role.

“The two Kevins are very good,” Trump says, referring to White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett and former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh.

“You have some other good people too, but I’ll be announcing something over the next couple of weeks,” Trump added.

data.title: Rio Tinto, BHP to join forces on Pilbara iron ore project
data.description:
Rio TInto (RIO) and BHP (BHP), the world's biggest mining companies, said Wednesday they will work together in Australia's Pilbara region to extract as much as 200M metric tons of iron ore from their neighboring Yandicoogina and Yandi operations.

Under two non-binding MoUs, the companies said they will explore the potential for collaboration on the development of Rio Tinto's (RIO) Wunbye deposit, and for BHP (BHP) to supply its Yandi Lower Channel Deposit ore to Rio for processing at its existing wet plants under agreed commercial terms.

The agreement builds on a previous agreement between Rio (RIO) and BHP (BHP) to mine the Mungadoo Pillar, which allowed mining of ore from the shared tenure boundary that had been inaccessible.

"By working smarter, we can better leverage existing infrastructure to unlock additional production with minimal capital requirements," said Matthew Holcz, the head of Rio's (RIO) iron ore operations. "Together we will extend the life of these operations, create additional value, and further support Western Australian jobs and local communities."

data.title: RF Industries anticipates continued sales growth into 2026 as diversification drives margin gains
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: RF Industries (RFIL) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Robert Dawson, CEO, highlighted a strong Q4 with net sales up 23% year-over-year to $22.7 million and declared that "gross profit margin of 37% exceeded our 30% target and adjusted EBITDA was 11.5% of net sales, above our stated goal of 10%." He emphasized that fiscal 2025 saw "net sales were $80.6 million, an increase of 24% compared to fiscal 2024," and that "from both the top line and bottom line perspective, fiscal '25 felt like a breakout year for RFI."
  • Dawson stated the company will "continue to execute our long-term strategic plan to further transform RFI from a product seller to a technology solutions provider," and is "intensely focused on diversifying end markets, driving further customer and market penetration and launching new products and solutions." He also noted the value created by moving away from customer concentration: "Now our results are healthier with diversity by product, customer and market."
  • COO Ray Bibisi reported, "We supported more than 130 projects across these categories delivering a meaningful contribution to revenue compared to prior years," and pointed to strong infrastructure activity, clear operational improvements, and a strong pipeline, especially with upcoming global events such as the L.A. Olympics and U.S. World Cup.
  • CFO Peter Yin stated, "Sales increased 23% to $22.7 million year-over-year and 15% on a sequential basis. Gross profit margin increased to 37% from 31% year-over-year" and highlighted, "Fourth quarter operating income was $903,000, a considerable improvement from the operating income of $96,000 we reported last year."

Outlook

  • Management did not provide specific numerical guidance but stated expectations for another year of sales growth in fiscal 2026. Dawson explained, "We expect some of the normal seasonality in Q1, we also expect to accelerate throughout the year in a similar trajectory to fiscal '25."
  • The company is focused on further market diversification, new product launches, and customer penetration. "We anticipate another year of sales growth," CEO Dawson said when discussing expectations for 2026.

Financial Results

  • Q4 net sales increased to $22.7 million, up 23% year-over-year and 15% sequentially, with a gross profit margin of 37% compared to 31% a year ago.
  • Full year revenue was $80.6 million, up 24% from the prior year, and gross profit margin for the year rose to 33% from 29%.
  • Q4 operating income was $903,000, net income was $174,000 or $0.02 per diluted share, and non-GAAP net income reached $2.1 million or $0.20 per diluted share. Q4 adjusted EBITDA was $2.6 million.
  • Working capital was $14.1 million, cash and cash equivalents totaled $5.1 million, and the company reduced net debt by $4.6 million year-over-year.
  • Backlog stood at $12.4 million as of the call, with management emphasizing that backlog is a snapshot and can vary significantly.

Q&A

  • Matthew Maus, B. Riley Securities, questioned growth trajectory and margin sustainability. CEO Dawson responded, "Our expectation for '26 is another year of growth...The joy of having a first quarter that includes November, December and January means you're always going to have seasonality."
  • Maus asked about gross margin drivers. Dawson explained, "It's really a nice combination of product and solution mix...but I can't really understate the strength of a sales number that starts to get up above $20 million a quarter."
  • On EBITDA targets, Dawson said, "The expectation for us is we got to find ways to keep it above that 10% number. That's not an easy feat."
  • Maus asked about new product impacts. Dawson cited integrated systems, DAC and small cell products, as well as legacy custom cabling.
  • An unnamed analyst asked about revenue mix, to which Dawson replied, "We're now seeing [telecom and wireless] more like 50%. About half of our sales are coming from things that I would call telecom and wireless. The remaining half is coming from...transportation, aerospace and defense, industrial and other OEM, public safety, and things like that."
  • Steven Kohl, Mangrove, inquired about capital allocation. Dawson stated, "At the moment, our priority is the same as it has been. We want to get that net debt as low as we can."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were positive and congratulatory, probing for details on growth sustainability, margin drivers, product mix, and capital allocation, reflecting increased confidence in the company's diversification and operational leverage.
  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing operational achievements and a strong outlook, while in Q&A sessions, responses remained constructive, occasionally cautious, particularly regarding guidance and cost pressures.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed increased optimism, with more focus on margin expansion and diversification benefits.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 saw an acceleration in growth and profitability compared to Q3, with net sales and margin gains surpassing those of the prior quarter.
  • The company shifted from a focus on steady growth in Q3 to highlighting a "breakout year" and a more diversified revenue base in Q4.
  • Margin improvement was more pronounced, with gross profit margin up to 37% from 34% in Q3, and adjusted EBITDA as a percent of sales above the 10% target for the first time.
  • Management tone shifted from cautious optimism in Q3 to confident celebration in Q4, while analysts increased focus on sustainability and capital allocation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited ongoing uncertainties and increased costs, including wage and benefits pressures, tariffs, and supply chain volatility, as factors to monitor.
  • There were questions regarding backlog variability and seasonality, with Dawson explaining, "Our backlog is a snapshot in time and can vary based on when orders are received and when orders are fulfilled."
  • The company continued to monitor interest rates and has renegotiated revolving credit facility terms to reduce costs.

Final Takeaway

RF Industries closed fiscal 2025 with significant revenue and margin growth, supported by increased diversification across markets and products. Management expects continued sales growth for 2026, driven by new product launches and further market penetration, while operational discipline and working capital improvements underpin a positive outlook. The company is positioned for ongoing profitability gains, with a strong pipeline and enhanced balance sheet flexibility entering the new fiscal year.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Oil tumbles after-hours as Trump signals U.S. might not attack Iran
data.description:
Crude oil futures settled higher Wednesday, then gave back most of the day's gains after-hours when President Trump said "the killing in Iran is stopping," an apparent sign of de-escalation between the U.S. and Iran.

Speaking at the White House, Trump told reporters that the U.S. had been notified Iran had "no plans to carry out any executions of protesters."

Asked if military action was off the table, Trump said, "We're going to watch it and see what the process is, but we were given a very good statement by people that are aware of what's going on."

U.S. WTI crude had surged 11% over the five trading days ending Wednesday while Trump reportedly weighed strikes on an increasingly unstable Iranian regime, but continuous futures contract quickly fell back more than 2% after the president's remarks eased fears of disruptions to Iranian supplies.

Earlier, prices had gained on fears of Iranian supply disruptions due to a potential U.S. attack on Iran and possible retaliation against U.S. military bases in the region, with front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for February delivery closing +1.4% to $62.02/bbl, its best settlement value since October 8, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for March delivery finishing +1.6% to $66.52/bbl, its highest close since September 30; it was the fifth consecutive daily gain for both benchmarks.

"The geopolitical factor continues to provide a formidable offset against deterioration in the global oil balances," Ritterbusch said in a note, with the worst case scenario "a U.S. attack that prompts Iran to shut down the Strait of Hormuz, but we continue to view this as a low probability occurrence."

Geopolitical concerns largely overshadowed the latest data from the Energy Information Administration, which showed U.S. crude inventories rose by 3.4M bbl/day in the week ended January 9, compared with expectations for a draw, remain ~3% below the five-year average for the time of year.

The EIA also reported gasoline inventories jumped by a nuch larger than expected 9M barrels, with gasoline stocks now ~4% above the five-year average, whiledistillate fuel inventories remained roughly flat, in line with expectations, and were ~4% below the five-year average.

The U.S. inventory data looked "bearish across the board," Ritterbusch wrote, as crude stocks rose by 3.4M barrels and gasoline stocks jumped by 9M barrels. "This sizable surplus appears poised to expand further with Gulf coast refinery activity still running full out."

U.S. natural gas futures fell ahead of weekly inventory data that is expected to show an increase in the storage surplus over the five-year average; front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for February delivery settled -8.7% at $3.120/MMBtu.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

data.title: Duke Energy's Florida unit to drop storm cost recovery charge from customer bills a month early
data.description:
Duke Energy (DUK) said Wednesday its Florida unit will remove the storm cost recovery charge related to the company's ~$1.1B response to hurricanes Debby, Helene and Milton from customer bills a month earlier than originally scheduled.

Duke (DUK) said residential customers can expect a ~$33 reduction on their monthly bills starting in February for every 1,000 kWh of electricity they use, and another ~$11 cut in March, bringing total reductions on customer bill to ~$44 from January levels.

Commercial and industrial customers also will see their monthly bills lowered between 9.6% and 15.8% from January levels, although the specific impact will vary based on usage and other factors.

Duke Energy Florida (DUK) owns 12,300 MW of energy capacity, supplying electricity to 2M residential, commercial and industrial customers across a 13,000 sq mile service area in the state.

data.title: Soho House jumps after securing $200M in alternative commitments to fund merger
data.description:
Soho House (SHCO) soared 13% in post-market trading after disclosing about $200 million in alternative commitments to fund the close of its planned merger.

The commitments include a $50 million equity letter from Morse Ventures, which is owned by the chairman/CEO of MCR Investors, according to an 8-K filing on Wednesday. MCR also notified Soho it will be committing $50 million in equity under the Original MCR Commitment for a total of $100 million in equity commitments from Morse Ventures and MCR. 

The alternative funding comes after Soho House (SHCO) shares slid 10% last Thursday after the company said MCR Hospitality Fund won't be able to fully fund  its $200 million commitment for the company’s pending sale.

Soho House (SHCO) in August agreed to be acquired for $9 a share by an investor group led by MCR Hotels.

data.title: Boeing flagged prior engine-mount failures before fatal UPS crash
data.description:
Boeing (BA) had flagged past failures of a structural component later found cracked on the cargo jet involved in November’s fatal United Parcel Service (UPS) crash, according to a mid-investigation update from U.S. safety officials.

Investigators with the National Transportation Safety Board on Wednesday said Boeing (BA) issued a service notice in early 2011. The notification alerted MD-11 operators to multiple prior failures of a spherical bearing race, a part designed to absorb stress and vibration in the engine mounting system. Boeing (BA) indicated at the time that inspections of the component would be added to routine maintenance checks.

The agency is now examining how those inspection recommendations were reflected in Boeing’s (BA) maintenance manuals and how UPS (UPS) applied them, along with any related communications between Boeing (BA) and the Federal Aviation Administration. Boeing (BA) previously concluded the issue didn’t pose an immediate flight safety risk.

Following the crash, the FAA temporarily grounded MD-11F cargo aircraft while operators conducted inspections and corrective actions, later extending those requirements to planes with similar engine-pylon designs.

A preliminary investigation found fatigue cracking in several engine-mount structures on the McDonnell Douglas MD-11F, which lost its left engine shortly after takeoff from Louisville. The aircraft failed to gain altitude and crashed moments later, killing the crew and people on the ground.

The MD-11, no longer produced, is now flown exclusively in cargo service.

data.title: Chevron expected to secure expanded Venezuela license from U.S. this week - Reuters
data.description:
Chevron (CVX) is expected to receive an expanded Venezuela license from the U.S. government this week that could allow for increased production and exports, Reuters reported Wednesday.

Chevron (CVX) likely will be one of several U.S. companies to secure approvals from the Trump administration to conduct business in Venezuela as oil companies, according to the report, which also said Marathon Petroleum (MPC) is in talks with U.S. officials to receive Venezuelan crude for its refineries, and that Valero Energy (VLO) and global traders Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) and Mercuria also have been in discussions for licenses to do business with Venezuela.

The U.S. has completed the first sales of Venezuelan oil that are part of a $2B deal between the two countries, and additional sales are expected in the coming days and weeks, Reuters also reported.

Chevron (CVX) and global trading houses Vitol and Trafigura reportedly have been competing for U.S. government deals to export the Venezuelan crude.

Chevron (CVX) is the only U.S. oil major currently producing crude in Venezuela, with ~240K bbl/day in joint ventures with state-run PDVSA; Chevron shares have gained nearly 9% since U.S. forces removed Venezuela's Maduro from power earlier this month.
